question.text: What must a Regulated Entity do as part of ongoing sanctions screening under the IFSCA Guidelines?

answer.text: Review customers, their business, and transactions against UN Security Council sanctions lists and other relevant sanctions lists
Screen only new customers at the time of onboarding
Screen only against domestic sanctions lists published by the Government of India
Screen only high-risk customers against international sanctions lists

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** A Regulated Entity shall review its customers, their business and transactions against United Nations Security Council sanctions lists and also against any other relevant sanctions list as part of ongoing CDD. This is a continuous obligation, not just at onboarding.
**The Trap:** Ongoing sanctions screening applies to ALL customers (not just high-risk), covers BOTH UNSC lists and other relevant lists, and is a CONTINUOUS obligation throughout the business relationship.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Sanctions screening is emphasized in IFSCA-01 more than Series XXIV because of IFSC's international operations. FATF Recommendations 6 and 7 mandate targeted financial sanctions.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Sanctions screening must be ongoing — not just at onboarding. UNSC lists are updated regularly. Any match must trigger immediate blocking/freezing and reporting to relevant authorities.
